title: Size of Equal Groups
essential_question: How can you model division to find how many in each group?
strategies: Deal out counters one at a time into each group until all are used.
Think: total ÷ number of groups = size of each group.

pretest.stem: 15 counters in 3 equal groups. How many in each?
21 counters in 7 equal groups. How many in each?
Ryan has 21 pencils for 3 holders equally. How many in each?
Corrine sets 24 plates on 6 tables equally. How many per table?
Alicia has 12 eggs for 4 recipes. Same number each. How many per recipe?

learn.title: Finding the Size of Each Group
learn.content: When you know the total and the number of groups, divide to find how many in each group. 28 ÷ 7 = 4 means 28 items in 7 groups = 4 in each.

learn.title: Using Counters
learn.content: Put out 7 circles. Deal 28 counters one at a time into each circle. Each circle gets 4 counters.

try.stem: 32 counters, 4 groups. How many in each?
hints: Think about what size of equal groups means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how size of equal groups works.
